 {"id":"bd-28db","title":"Add 'bd status' command for issue database overview","description":"Implement a bd status command that provides a quick snapshot of the issue database state, similar to how git status shows working tree state.

Expected output: Show summary including counts by state (open, in-progress, blocked, closed), recent activity (last 7 days), and quick overview without needing multiple queries.

Example output showing issue counts, recent activity stats, and pointer to bd list for details.

Proposed options: --all (show all issues), --assigned (show issues assigned to current user), --json (JSON format output)

Use cases: Quick project health check, onboarding for new contributors, integration with shell prompts or CI/CD, daily standup reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-02T17:25:59.203549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:54:00.205191-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:54:00.205191-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - bd status shows summary and activity"}

+{"id":"bd-2l03","title":"Implement await type handlers (gh:run, gh:pr, timer, human, mail)","description":"Implement condition checking for each await type.

## Handlers Needed
- gh:run:<id> - Check GitHub Actions run status via gh CLI
- gh:pr:<id> - Check PR merged/closed status via gh CLI  
- timer:<duration> - Simple elapsed time check
- human:<prompt> - Check for human approval (via mail?)
- mail:<pattern> - Check for mail matching pattern

## Implementation Location
This is Deacon logic, so likely in Gas Town (gt) not beads.

## Interface
```go
type AwaitHandler interface {
    Check(awaitID string) (completed bool, result string, err error)
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:38.492837-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:38.492837-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2l03","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:52.990746-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-2l03","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.510792-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2q6d","title":"Beads commands operate on stale database without warning","description":"All beads read operations should validate database is in sync with JSONL before proceeding.

**Current Behavior:**
- Commands can query/read from stale database
- Only mutation operations (like 'bd sync') check if JSONL is newer
- User gets incorrect results without realizing database is out of sync

**Expected Behavior:**
- All beads commands should have pre-flight check for database freshness
- If JSONL is newer than database, refuse to operate with error: "Database out of sync. Run 'bd import' first."
- Same safety check that exists for 'bd sync' should apply to ALL operations

**Impact:**
- Users make decisions based on incomplete/outdated data
- Silent failures lead to confusion (e.g., thinking issues don't exist when they do)
- Similar to running git commands on stale repo without being warned to pull

**Example:**
- Searched for bd-g9eu issue file: not found
- Issue exists in .beads/issues.jsonl (in git)
- Database was stale, but no warning was given
- Led to incorrect conclusion that issue was already closed/deleted","notes":"## Implementation Complete

**Phase 1: Created staleness check (cmd/bd/staleness.go)**
- ensureDatabaseFresh() function checks JSONL mtime vs last_import_time
- Returns error with helpful message when database is stale
- Auto-skips in daemon mode (daemon has auto-import)

**Phase 2: Added to all read commands**
- list, show, ready, status, stale, info, duplicates, validate
- Check runs before database queries in direct mode
- Daemon mode already protected via checkAndAutoImportIfStale()

**Phase 3: Code Review Findings**
See follow-up issues:
- bd-XXXX: Add warning when staleness check errors
- bd-YYYY: Improve CheckStaleness error handling
- bd-ZZZZ: Refactor redundant daemon checks (low priority)

**Testing:**
- Build successful: go build ./cmd/bd
- Binary works: ./bd --version
- Ready for manual testing

**Next Steps:**
1. Test with stale database scenario
2. Implement review improvements
3. Close issue when tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T19:33:40.019297-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.535149-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T19:11:12.982639-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-2vh3","title":"Ephemeral issue cleanup and history compaction","description":"## Problem

Beads history grows without bound. Every message, handoff, work assignment
stays in issues.jsonl forever. Enterprise users will balk at "git as database."

## Solution: Two-Tier Cleanup

### Tier 1: Ephemeral Cleanup (v1)

bd cleanup --ephemeral --closed

- Deletes closed issues where ephemeral=true from issues.jsonl
- Safe: only removes explicitly marked ephemeral + closed
- Preserves git history (commits still exist)
- Run after swarm completion

### Tier 2: History Compaction (v2)

bd compact --squash

- Rewrites issues.jsonl to remove tombstones
- Optionally squashes git history (interactive rebase equivalent)
- Preserves Merkle proofs for deleted items
- Advanced: cold storage tiering

## HOP Context

| Layer | HOP Role | Persistence |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| Execution trace | None | Ephemeral |
| Work scaffolding | None | Summarizable |
| Work outcome | CV entry | Permanent |
| Validation record | Stake proof | Permanent |

"Execution is ephemeral. Outcomes are permanent. You can't squash your CV."

## Success Criteria

- After cleanup --ephemeral: issues.jsonl only contains persistent work
- Work outcomes preserved (CV entries)
- Validation records preserved (stake proofs)
- Execution scaffolding removed (transient coordination)","notes":"## Implementation Plan (REVISED after code review)

See history/EPHEMERAL_MOLECULES_DESIGN.md for comprehensive design + review.

## Key Simplification

After code review, Tier 1 is MUCH simpler than originally designed:

- **Original**: Separate ephemeral repo with routing.ephemeral config
- **Revised**: Just set Wisp: true in cloneSubgraph()

The wisp field and bd cleanup --wisp already exist\!

## Child Tasks (in dependency order)

1. **bd-2vh3.2**: Tier 1 - Ephemeral spawning (SIMPLIFIED) [READY]
   - Just add Wisp: true to template.go:474
   - Add --persistent flag to opt out
2. **bd-2vh3.3**: Tier 2 - Basic bd mol squash command
3. **bd-2vh3.4**: Tier 3 - AI-powered squash summarization
4. **bd-2vh3.5**: Tier 4 - Auto-squash on molecule completion
5. **bd-2vh3.6**: Tier 5 - JSONL archive rotation (DEFERRED: post-1.0)

## What Already Exists

| Component | Location |
|-----------|----------|
| Ephemeral field | internal/types/types.go:45 |
| bd cleanup --wisp | cmd/bd/cleanup.go:72 |
| cloneSubgraph() | cmd/bd/template.go:456 |
| loadTemplateSubgraph() | cmd/bd/template.go |

## HOP Alignment

'Execution is ephemeral. Outcomes are permanent. You can't squash your CV.'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:02:20.101367-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:50:02.958155-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:50:02.958155-08:00","close_reason":"Core feature complete: bd cleanup --wisp works, molecules marked as wisps. bd-2vh3.6 (archive rotation) deferred post-1.0."}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.1","title":"Tier 1: Ephemeral repo routing","description":"Add routing.ephemeral config option to route ephemeral=true issues to separate location.

## Changes Required

1. Add `routing.ephemeral` config option (default: empty = disabled)
2. Update routing logic in `determineRepo()` to check ephemeral flag
3. Update `bd create` to respect ephemeral routing
4. Update import/export for multi-location support
5. Ephemeral repo can be:
   - Separate git repo (~/.beads-ephemeral)
   - Non-git directory (just filesystem)
   - Same repo, different branch (future)

## Config

```bash
bd config set routing.ephemeral "~/.beads-ephemeral"
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bd create "test" --ephemeral` creates in ephemeral repo when configured
- `bd list` shows issues from both repos
- Ephemeral repo never synced to remote","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:26.648052-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:01.815357-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:01.815357-08:00","deleted_by":"stevey","delete_reason":"manual delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.2","title":"Tier 1: Ephemeral repo routing","description":"Simplified: Make mol spawn set ephemeral=true on spawned issues.

## The Fix

Modify cloneSubgraph() in template.go to set Ephemeral: true:

```go
// template.go:474
newIssue := &types.Issue{
    Title:      substituteVariables(oldIssue.Title, vars),
    // ... existing fields ...
    Ephemeral:  true,  // ADD THIS LINE
}
```

## Optional: Add --persistent flag

Add flag to bd mol spawn for when you want spawned issues to persist:

```bash
bd mol spawn mol-code-review --var pr=123  # ephemeral (default)
bd mol spawn mol-code-review --var pr=123 --persistent  # not ephemeral
```

## Why This Is Simpler Than Original Design

Original design proposed separate ephemeral repo routing. After code review:

- Ephemeral field already exists in schema
- bd cleanup --ephemeral already works
- No new config needed
- No multi-repo complexity

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol spawn creates issues with ephemeral=true
- bd cleanup --ephemeral -f deletes them after closing
- --persistent flag opts out of ephemeral
- Existing molecules continue to work","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:36.661604-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:43:22.990244-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:43:22.990244-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: mol spawn now creates ephemeral issues by default. Use --persistent to opt out.  {"id":"bd-iq19","title":"Distill: promote ad-hoc epic to proto","description":"Extract a reusable proto from an existing ad-hoc epic.

COMMAND: bd mol distill <epic-id> [--as <proto-name>]

BEHAVIOR:
- Clone the epic and all children as a new proto
- Set is_template=true on all cloned issues
- Replace concrete values with {{variable}} placeholders (interactive or --var flags)
- Add to proto catalog

FLAGS:
- --as NAME: Custom proto ID (default: proto-<epic-id>)
- --var field=placeholder: Replace value with variable placeholder
- --interactive: Prompt for each field that looks parameterizable
- --dry-run: Preview the proto structure

EXAMPLE:
  bd mol distill bd-o5xe --as proto-feature-workflow \
    --var title=feature_name \
    --var assignee=worker

USE CASES:
- Team develops good workflow organically, wants to reuse it
- Capture tribal knowledge as executable templates
- Create starting point for similar future work

The reverse of spawn: instead of proto → molecule, it's molecule → proto.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:05:07.953538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:31:56.814246-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T10:31:56.814246-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented distill command in mol.go","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iq19","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:05:16.495774-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-iq19","depends_on_id":"bd-rnnr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:05:16.560404-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iq7n","title":"Audit and fix JSONL filename mismatches across all repo clones","description":"## Problem

Multiple clones of repos are configured with different JSONL filenames (issues.jsonl vs beads.jsonl), causing:
1. JSONL files to be resurrected after deletion (one clone pushes issues.jsonl, another pushes beads.jsonl)
2. Agents unable to see issues filed by other agents after sync
3. Merge conflicts and data inconsistencies

## Root Cause

When repos were "bd doctored" or initialized at different times, some got issues.jsonl (old default) and others got beads.jsonl (Beads repo specific). These clones push their respective files, creating duplicates.

## Task

Scan all repo clones under ~/src/ (1-2 levels deep) and standardize their JSONL configuration.

### Step 1: Find all beads-enabled repos

```bash
# Find all directories named 'beads' at levels 1-2 under ~/src/
find ~/src -maxdepth 2 -type d -name beads
```

### Step 2: For each repo found, check configuration

For each directory from Step 1, check:
- Does `.beads/metadata.json` exist?
- What is the `jsonl_export` value?
- What JSONL files actually exist in `.beads/`?
- Are there multiple JSONL files (problem!)?

### Step 3: Create audit report

Generate a report showing:
```
Repo Path                           | Config        | Actual Files           | Status
----------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------- | --------
~/src/beads                         | beads.jsonl   | beads.jsonl            | OK
~/src/dave/beads                    | issues.jsonl  | issues.jsonl           | MISMATCH
~/src/emma/beads                    | issues.jsonl  | issues.jsonl, beads.jsonl | DUPLICATE!
```

### Step 4: Determine canonical name for each repo

For repos that are the SAME git repository (check `git remote -v`):
- Group them together
- Determine which JSONL filename should be canonical (majority wins, or beads.jsonl for the beads repo itself)
- List which clones need to be updated

### Step 5: Generate fix script

Create a script that for each mismatched clone:
1. Updates `.beads/metadata.json` to use the canonical name
2. If JSONL file needs renaming: `git mv .beads/old.jsonl .beads/new.jsonl`
3. Removes any duplicate JSONL files: `git rm .beads/duplicate.jsonl`
4. Commits the change
5. Syncs: `bd sync`

### Expected Output

1. Audit report showing all repos and their config status
2. List of repos grouped by git remote (same repository)
3. Fix script or manual instructions for standardizing each repo
4. Verification that after fixes, all clones of the same repo use the same JSONL filename

### Edge Cases

- Handle repos without metadata.json (use default discovery)
- Handle repos with no git remote (standalone/local)
- Handle repos that are not git repositories
- Don't modify repos with uncommitted changes (warn instead)

### Success Criteria

- All clones of the same git repository use the same JSONL filename
- No duplicate JSONL files in any repo
- All configurations documented in metadata.json
- bd doctor passes on all repos","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-21T23:58:35.044762-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.531403-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:50:59.510972-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-is6m","title":"Add gate checking to Deacon patrol loop","description":"Integrate gate checking into Deacon's patrol cycle.

## Patrol Integration
```go
func (d *Deacon) checkGates(ctx context.Context) {
    gates, _ := d.store.ListOpenGates(ctx)
    
    for _, gate := range gates {
        // Check timeout
        if time.Since(gate.CreatedAt) > gate.Timeout {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "timeout")
            d.closeGate(gate, "timed out")
            continue
        }
        
        // Check condition
        if d.checkCondition(gate.AwaitType, gate.AwaitID) {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "cleared")
            d.closeGate(gate, "condition met")
        }
    }
}
```

## Note
This task is in Gas Town (gt), not beads. May need to be moved there.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:36.839709-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:36.839709-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-is6m","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:52.909253-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-is6m","depends_on_id":"bd-u66e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.428084-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iw4z","title":"Compound visualization in bd mol show","description":"Enhance bd mol show to display compound structure.

ENHANCEMENTS:
- Show constituent protos and how they're bonded
- Display bond type (sequential/parallel) between components
- Indicate attachment points
- Show combined variable requirements across all protos

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:

  Compound: proto-feature-with-tests
  Bonded from:
    └─ proto-feature (root)
       └─ proto-testing (sequential, after completion)
  
  Variables: {{name}}, {{version}}, {{test_suite}}
  
  Structure:
    proto-feature-with-tests
    ├─ Design feature {{name}}
    ├─ Implement core
    ├─ Write unit tests      ← from proto-testing
    └─ Run test suite {{test_suite}}  ← from proto-testing","status":"deferred","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:26.71318-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:12:44.012871-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iw4z","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.500865-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-iw4z","depends_on_id":"bd-rnnr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.891643-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-j0tr","title":"Phase 1.3: Basic TOON read/write operations","description":"Add basic TOON read/write operations to bdt executable. Implement create, list, and show commands that use the internal/toon package for encoding/decoding to TOON format.

## Subtasks
1. Implement bdt create command - Create issues and serialize to TOON format
2. Implement bdt list command - Read issues.toon and display all issues
3. Implement bdt show command - Display single issue by ID
4. Add file I/O operations for issues.toon
5. Integrate internal/toon package (EncodeTOON, DecodeJSON)
6. Write tests for create, list, show operations

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/bdt/create.go - Create command
- cmd/bdt/list.go - List command  
- cmd/bdt/show.go - Show command
- cmd/bdt/storage.go - File I/O helper

## Success Criteria
- bdt create "Issue title" creates and saves to issues.toon
- bdt list displays all issues in human-readable format
- bdt list --json shows JSON output
- bdt show <id> displays single issue
- Issues round-trip correctly: create → list → show

 {"id":"bd-j3il","title":"Add bd reset command for clean slate restart","description":"Implement a command to reset beads to a clean starting state.

**Context:** GitHub issue #479 - users sometimes get beads into an invalid state after updates, and there's no clean way to start fresh. The git backup/restore mechanism that protects against accidental deletion also makes it hard to intentionally reset.

**Current workaround** (from maphew):
```bash
bd daemons killall
git rm .beads/*.jsonl
git commit -m 'remove old issues'
rm .beads/*
bd init
bd onboard
```

**Desired:** A proper `bd reset` command that handles this cleanly and safely.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T08:41:34.956552+11:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T08:43:49.970591+11:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T08:43:49.970591+11:00"}

 {"id":"bd-lw0x","title":"Fix bd sync race condition with daemon causing dirty working directory","description":"After bd sync completes with sync.branch mode, subsequent bd commands or daemon file watcher would see a hash mismatch and trigger auto-import, which then schedules re-export, dirtying the working directory.

**Root cause:**
1. bd sync exports JSONL with NEW content (hash H1)
2. bd sync updates jsonl_content_hash = H1 in DB
3. bd sync restores JSONL from HEAD (OLD content, hash H0)
4. Now: file hash = H0, DB hash = H1 (MISMATCH)
5. Daemon or next CLI command sees mismatch, imports from OLD JSONL
6. Import triggers re-export → file is dirty

**Fix:**
After restoreBeadsDirFromBranch(), update jsonl_content_hash to match the restored file's hash. This ensures daemon and CLI see file hash = DB hash → no spurious import/export cycle.

Related: bd-c83r (multiple daemon prevention)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T06:42:17.130839-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T06:43:33.329042-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T06:43:33.329042-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-lxzx","title":"Add close_reason to JSONL export format documentation","description":"PR #551 now persists close_reason to the database, but there's a question about whether this field should be exported to JSONL format.

## Current State
- close_reason is stored in issues.close_reason column
- close_reason is also stored in events table (audit trail)
- The JSONL export format may or may not include close_reason

## Questions
1. Should close_reason be exported to JSONL format?
2. If yes, where should it go (root level or nested in events)?
3. Should there be any special handling to avoid duplication?
4. How should close_reason be handled during JSONL import?

## Why This Matters
- JSONL is the git-friendly sync format
- Other beads instances import from JSONL
- close_reason is meaningful data that should be preserved across clones

## Suggested Action
- Check if close_reason is currently exported in JSONL
- If not, add it to the export schema
- Document the field in JSONL format spec
- Add tests for round-trip (export -> import -> verify close_reason)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.414916-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.414916-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lxzx","depends_on_id":"bd-z86n","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:25:17.416131-08:00","created_by":"stevey","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"bd-rp4o","title":"Deleted issues resurrect during bd sync (tombstones not propagating)","description":"## Problem

When issues are deleted with bd delete --force, they get deleted from the local DB but resurrect during the next bd sync.

## Reproduction

1. Observe orphan warnings (bd-cb64c226.*, bd-cbed9619.*)
2. Delete them: bd delete bd-cb64c226.1 ... --force
3. Run bd sync
4. Orphan warnings reappear - issues were resurrected!

## Root Cause Hypothesis

The sync branch workflow (beads-sync) has the old state before deletions. When bd sync pulls from beads-sync and copies JSONL to main, the deleted issues are re-imported.

Tombstones may not be properly:
1. Written to beads-sync during export
2. Propagated during pull/merge
3. Honored during import

## Related

- bd-7b7h: chicken-and-egg sync.branch bug (same workflow)
- bd-ncwo: ID-based fallback matching to prevent ghost resurrection

## Files to Investigate

- cmd/bd/sync.go (export/import flow)
- internal/syncbranch/worktree.go (PullFromSyncBranch, copyJSONLToMainRepo)
- internal/importer/ (tombstone handling)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T23:09:43.072696-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

 {"id":"bd-u2sc.2","title":"Migrate sort.Slice to slices.SortFunc","description":"Go 1.21+ provides slices.SortFunc which is cleaner and slightly faster than sort.Slice.

Found 15+ instances of sort.Slice in:
- cmd/bd/autoflush.go
- cmd/bd/count.go
- cmd/bd/daemon_sync.go
- cmd/bd/doctor.go
- cmd/bd/export.go
- cmd/bd/import.go
- cmd/bd/integrity.go
- cmd/bd/jira.go
- cmd/bd/list.go
- cmd/bd/migrate_hash_ids.go
- cmd/bd/rename_prefix.go
- cmd/bd/show.go

Example migration:
```go
// Before
sort.Slice(issues, func(i, j int) bool {
    return issues[i].Priority < issues[j].Priority
})

// After
slices.SortFunc(issues, func(a, b *types.Issue) int {
    return cmp.Compare(a.Priority, b.Priority)
})
```

Benefits:
- Cleaner 3-way comparison
- Slightly better performance
- Modern idiomatic Go","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573524-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:10:12.639807-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:10:12.639807-08:00","close_reason":"Migrated all 19 instances of sort.Slice to slices.SortFunc across 17 files in cmd/bd/. Used cmp.Compare for orderable types, time.Compare for time comparisons, and cmp.Or for multi-field sorts.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573978-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-u2sc.4","title":"Introduce slog for structured daemon logging","description":"The daemon currently uses fmt.Fprintf for logging which produces unstructured output. Migrating to slog would provide:

- Structured, parseable logs (JSON format option)
- Log levels (debug, info, warn, error)
- Context propagation
- Better log aggregation

Files to update:
- cmd/bd/daemon_logger.go
- Debug output throughout daemon code

Example:
```go
// Before
fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to detect user role: %v\n", err)

// After
slog.Warn("failed to detect user role", "error", err)
```

This is particularly valuable for daemon troubleshooting since daemon logs are often reviewed after the fact.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:16.47144-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:16.47144-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:16.471878-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

# Check gate status
bd gate show <id>

# List open gates
bd gate list

# Close gate (usually done by Deacon)
bd gate close <id> --reason "completed"

# Add waiter to existing gate
bd gate wait <id> --notify <addr>
```

- Gate list filters for open gates
- Gate wait adds to waiters[] array

+{"id":"bd-udsi","title":"Async Gates for Agent Coordination","description":"Agents need an async primitive for waiting on external events (CI completion, API responses, human approval). Gates are wisp issues that block until external conditions are met, managed by the Deacon.

## Core Concepts

**Gate** = wisp issue that blocks until external condition is met
- Type: gate
- Phase: wisp (never synced, ephemeral)
- Assignee: deacon/ (Deacon monitors it)
- Fields: await_type, await_id, timeout, waiters[]

**Await Types:**
- gh:run:<id> - GitHub Actions run completion
- gh:pr:<id> - PR merged/closed
- timer:<duration> - Simple delay
- human:<prompt> - Human approval required
- mail:<pattern> - Wait for mail matching pattern

## Open Questions
- Should gates live in wisp storage or main storage with wisp flag?
- Do we need a gate catalog (like molecule catalog)?
- Should waits-for dep type work with gates?","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:02.711062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:02.711062-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-umbf","title":"Design contributor namespace isolation for beads pollution prevention","description":"## Problem

When contributors work on beads-the-project using beads-the-tool, their personal work-tracking issues leak into PRs. The .beads/issues.jsonl is intentionally tracked (it's the project's issue database), but contributors' local issues pollute the diff.

This is a recursion problem unique to self-hosting projects.

## Possible Solutions to Explore

1. **Contributor namespaces** - Each contributor gets a private prefix (e.g., `bd-steve-xxxx`) that's gitignored or filtered
2. **Separate database** - Contributors use BEADS_DIR pointing elsewhere for personal tracking
3. **Issue ownership/visibility flags** - Mark issues as "local-only" vs "project"
4. **Prefix-based filtering** - Configure which prefixes are committed vs ignored

## Design Considerations

- Should be zero-friction for contributors (no manual setup)
- Must not break existing workflows
- Needs to work with sync/collaboration features
- Consider: what if a "personal" issue graduates to "project" issue?

## Expansion Needed

This is a placeholder. Needs detailed design exploration before implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:29.638743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:41.345673-08:00"}

- Display what will be fixed and ask user to confirm (Y/n) before proceeding
- Support fixing issues like:
  - Missing or broken git hooks
  - Sync problems with remote
  - File permission issues
  - Any other auto-repairable issues doctor detects

Implementation notes:
- Maintain separation between detection (existing doctor code) and repair (new fix code)
- Each fix should be idempotent and safe to run multiple times
- Provide clear output about what was fixed
- Log any fixes that fail with actionable error messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:48.411264-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:58.88609-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-ykqu","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: "Gate timed out"
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Escalation Path
- Witness sees stuck worker, nudges them
- Worker can escalate to human if needed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:53.072862-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.595085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
